vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1568

plan.title: swarm: ask operator for subagent backend during interactive setup

narratives.Description: Interactive swarm setup currently lets backend selection surface as a late headless-launch failure when plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend is unset. This story makes the swarm skill ask the operator for their intended subagent backend during setup, distinguishing preference from probe availability before launch.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary

Directive's interactive swarm setup should ask the operator which subagent backend they want before it reaches headless launch. Today, if `plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend` is unset, the first clear backend-selection moment can appear as a `task swarm:launch` failure that lists probed backends. That makes the available cloud backend look like a default or recommendation, even when the operator intended local Composer/Cursor agents.

## Problem

The backend guard is correct for autonomous/headless dispatch: it should fail closed when no backend is selected. But the interactive swarm skill has enough context to ask earlier, during allocation/setup, before launch planning hardens.

A better operator experience is:

- Detect `plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend` is unset during interactive swarm setup.
- Present the stable backend choices and probe status in a deterministic numbered menu.
- Ask the operator whether to use local Composer/Cursor subagents, Cursor cloud agents, Grok Build, or discuss/back.
- Persist the selected backend when appropriate, or record a per-run launch-context choice when the operator does not want to update project policy.
- Avoid implying that the only probe-available backend is the recommended backend.

## Acceptance criteria

- Interactive swarm setup asks for a subagent backend before headless `task swarm:launch` is attempted when `plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend` is unset.
- The prompt distinguishes operator preference from probe availability.
- The prompt follows the deterministic questions contract: visible numbered options, `Discuss` and `Back` as the final two options.
- Autonomous/headless launch remains fail-closed when backend policy/launch context is missing.
- Regression coverage pins the swarm skill guidance so future changes do not move backend selection back to a late failure message only.

## Notes

This should be part of the current swarm so the next operator-run swarm can test the improved setup behavior.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md Phase 0 / Phase 1 setup guidance so the interactive path runs task policy:subagent-backends, detects an unset plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend, and presents a deterministic numbered backend-selection menu before any task swarm:launch call.
2. Preserve the documented headless failure path in scripts/swarm_launch.py by treating it as existing behavior to verify, not as a prompt surface; the story should not change autonomous launch into an interactive prompt.
3. Add or update tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py to assert the swarm skill names task policy:subagent-backends, distinguishes operator preference from probe availability, keeps Discuss and Back as the final backend prompt options, and explicitly says cursor-cloud is not an implicit default just because it is probe-available.
4. If the deterministic menu contract needs cross-reference coverage, update tests/content/test_deterministic_questions.py to assert backend-selection prompts follow visible numbered-menu rules. Verification evidence: run uv run pytest tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py tests/content/test_deterministic_questions.py.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm operator, I want Directive to ask which subagent backend I intend to use before launch, so that cloud availability is not mistaken for a default when I want local agents.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1568; skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md backend policy surface; task policy:subagent-backends; task swarm:launch fail-closed guard.

items.title: Interactive swarm setup asks for backend before launch

narrative.Acceptance: Given plan.policy.swarmSubagentBackend is unset, when the operator is using the interactive swarm setup path, then the skill asks for the intended subagent backend before attempting headless task swarm:launch.

Given backend probes report only cursor-cloud as available, when the backend prompt is rendered, then it does not imply cursor-cloud is the recommended/default choice and clearly lets the operator choose local Composer/Cursor, Cursor cloud, Grok Build, Discuss, or Back.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1568 operator preference versus probe availability.

narrative.Acceptance: Given autonomous/headless task swarm:launch runs without backend policy or launch-context selection, when launch validates backend policy, then it still fails closed with an actionable selection message.

items.title: Content tests pin deterministic backend prompt guidance

narrative.Acceptance: Given content tests run, then they fail if the swarm skill drops the early backend-selection step, visible numbered options, or Discuss/Back finality.

references.title: Issue #1568: swarm: ask operator for subagent backend during interactive setup

expected_outputs: content tests assert interactive swarm asks for backend early while headless launch remains fail-closed
